Based on announcements from Google I/O 2026, Google Antigravity 2.0 is the major upcoming platform update, shifting from an IDE plugin to a standalone, agent-first desktop application designed for multi-agent orchestration. blog.google +1Here are the key upcoming updates and model changes as of late May 2026: Key Model & Efficiency Updates (Gemini 3.5 Flash) Gemini 3.5 Flash Integration: The core model is now Gemini 3.5 Flash, which is faster and more efficient, reducing token usage by approximately 45% for simple tasks.Model Tiering: Google is offering a choice between "Low" (Flash) for speed/efficiency and "Medium" for more complex tasks, with a "3.5 Pro" model scheduled for release in June 2026.Improved Reasoning: The new models are designed to handle complex workflows, such as refactoring legacy authentication modules or building interactive data visualizations. YouTube·Julian Goldie SEO +3Antigravity 2.0 Platform Features "Agent-First" Interface: The 2.0 update features a redesigned UI focusing on agent conversations (left panel) and project management.Managed Agents in Gemini API: Users can now invoke agents via the API to execute code in isolated Linux environments, allowing for "sub-agent" workflows where agents break down tasks autonomously.Scheduled Tasks: Agents can now be scheduled to run pre-specified tasks using cron scheduling.Native Voice & Integration: Live voice transcription is integrated using Gemini Audio models. Furthermore, new skills are available for Android developers (CLI), Firebase, and web development. blog.google +3Upcoming Changes & Transition Notices Agent Manager Remo
